events.title: (Re)sounding: Art Exhibition
events.description: Sound is not simply something we hear, but something we inhabit, shape, and are shaped by. Through immersive audio installations, evocative visual translations of sound, and interactive artworks that invite participation, the artists in (Re)sounding explore sound as a dynamic subject and material. This exhibition is an invitation to listen more deeply—to feel the weight and resonance of sound, to dwell inside it, and to hear the world in new ways.

events.description: Step beyond Main Street and experience Park City with a local! This guided tour blends mountain-town charm, historic buildings, Olympic legacy, art-filled tunnels, and more! Gain an insider perspective through a small-group setting designed to help you feel truly connected to Park City. We'll meet upstairs inside Old Town Transit Center (easily accessed by Park City's fare-free bus!) to wander for 1-1.5 hours and just under a mile. Weather-appropriate layers and a water bottle are recommended, families are encouraged, and gratuities are appreciated. Use code PR20 for 20% off!

events.description: Park City Museum will host a lecture called Medical Care in Park City in 1900—Not For the Faint of Heart given by Dr. Thomas Schwenk on Wednesday, June 3, 2026 from 5 to 6 p.m. at the Park City Museum Education and Collections Center located at 2079 Sidewinder Drive, Park City UT. Life was rough and death common in the remote mining town of Park City in the late 1800s and early 1900s. This presentation will discuss common diseases and injuries of the era, the doctors and nurses who cared for the town, and the funding and building of the much-needed Miners Hospital in 1904. events.title: painting with wool
events.description: painting with wool Thursday, June 4th | 6 PM | Our Lady of the Snows | $15 | Sign-up Required In this class, students will learn how to needle felt using a single felting needle and a variety of colorful wool fibers to create a 2D wool painting. Wool painting is done by pushing wool fibers into a flat piece of wool felt with a foam pad underneath. Students will learn how to blend wool colors and a number of techniques for forming desired shapes and textures with the wool. If you enjoy creating with repetitive motion and a medium that is easy to adjust and learn with, this class is for you! ...

events.title: The Great Weed Roundup
events.description: Summit Land Conservancy and Summit County are looking for motivated volunteers to help us uproot noxious weeds! With spring fully in swing, join us for our next Stewardship Saturday at Miss Billie’s Preserve (Summit Community Gardens)! We will be pulling invasive dyer's woad, thistle, and white top while they are in bloom and before they can release their seeds. These are all aggressive, introduced plants that grow rapidly and create dense thickets which crowd out our native grasses and wildflowers. When we remove these weeds, we help to restore our local grassland ecosystem and ensure th...
events.location: Miss Billie's Preserve, Park City, UT

events.description: A joyful immersion in music, mantras, and dance Let Your Heart Soar Kirtan is a joyous form of meditation that combines music with hearing and singing sacred mantras. Singing these powerful transcendental sounds together frees our mind of stress and anxiety and awakens a deep sense of peace, connection, and joy. The Kirtan Experience is the work of a growing collective of meditators, musicians, artists and friends from around the world who have been deeply touched by this practice and feel inspired to share it with others. events.description: Step beyond Main Street and experience Park City with a local! This guided tour blends mountain-town charm, historic buildings, Olympic legacy, art-filled tunnels, and more! Gain an insider perspective through a small-group setting designed to help you feel truly connected to Park City. We'll meet upstairs inside Old Town Transit Center (easily accessed by Park City's fare-free bus!) to wander for 1-1.5 hours and just under a mile. Weather-appropriate layers and a water bottle are recommended, families are encouraged, and gratuities are appreciated. Use code PR20 for 20% off!

events.title: Old Town walking tour
events.description: Step beyond Main Street and experience Park City with a local! This guided tour blends mountain-town charm, historic buildings, Olympic legacy, art-filled tunnels, and more! Gain an insider perspective through a small-group setting designed to help you feel truly connected to Park City. We'll meet upstairs inside Old Town Transit Center (easily accessed by Park City's fare-free bus!) to wander for 1-1.5 hours and just under a mile. Weather-appropriate layers and a water bottle are recommended, families are encouraged, and gratuities are appreciated. Use code PR20 for 20% off!
events.location: Old Town Transit Center, Park City, UT

events.title: Old Town walking tour
events.description: Step beyond Main Street and experience Park City with a local! This guided tour blends mountain-town charm, historic buildings, Olympic legacy, art-filled tunnels, and more! Gain an insider perspective through a small-group setting designed to help you feel truly connected to Park City. We'll meet upstairs inside Old Town Transit Center (easily accessed by Park City's fare-free bus!) to wander for 1-1.5 hours and just under a mile. Weather-appropriate layers and a water bottle are recommended, families are encouraged, and gratuities are appreciated. Use code PR20 for 20% off!

events.title: Old Town walking tour
events.description: Step beyond Main Street and experience Park City with a local! This guided tour blends mountain-town charm, historic buildings, Olympic legacy, art-filled tunnels, and more! Gain an insider perspective through a small-group setting designed to help you feel truly connected to Park City. We'll meet upstairs inside Old Town Transit Center (easily accessed by Park City's fare-free bus!) to wander for 1-1.5 hours and just under a mile. Weather-appropriate layers and a water bottle are recommended, families are encouraged, and gratuities are appreciated. Use code PR20 for 20% off!
events.location: Old Town Transit Center, Park City, UT

events.title: Main Street Walking Tour
events.description: Put on your walking shoes and explore Park City’s Historic Main Street! Explore the architecture and stories of Park City’s unique history-from the days as a booming mine town to an emerging ski town. The Park City Museum’s popular Historic Main Street Public Walking Tours are Monday through Friday at 2 p.m. from June 29 to September 19. Meet at the Museum store. Cost is $15 per person. No tours on August 8 \(Kimball Arts Festival\) and September 7 \(Labor Day/Miners’ Day\). The tour is most appropriate for ages 13 and up. events.title: Park City Trail Series 10K
events.description: Fill your summer calendar with trail races! Park City Trail Series is a 3-race series that runs from June – August. It starts with a 5K and works up to a half marathon. The courses are forgiving enough for new trail runners but bring the excitement that seasoned trail runners love. You can mix and match which races you would like to sign up for or get all three at an amazing price! Plus, you can 10% per person by signing up 4 or more people for the series. So, get your family, friends, neighbors, or whoever you need to recruit, and get signed up today! THERE ARE NO DEFERRALS OR REFUNDS.

events.description: Put on your walking shoes and explore Park City’s Historic Main Street! Explore the architecture and stories of Park City’s unique history-from the days as a booming mine town to an emerging ski town. The Park City Museum’s popular Historic Main Street Public Walking Tours are Monday through Friday at 2 p.m. from June 29 to September 19. Meet at the Museum store. Cost is $15 per person. No tours on August 8 \(Kimball Arts Festival\) and September 7 \(Labor Day/Miners’ Day\). The tour is most appropriate for ages 13 and up. Please arrive 10 minutes early, wear comfortable walking shoes, ...

events.title: Kenneth Peloke: Year of the Horse
events.description: Working in oil on large-format canvases, Kenneth Peloke approaches the horse not as subject but as a vessel for energy, instinct, and the kind of presence that resists easy translation into paint. His process is physical and deliberate, built through layers of mark-making that accumulate into something that feels less rendered than conjured. Year of the Horse brings together new works that explore what the animal has always represented in the human imagination: freedom, strength, the untamed thing we are drawn to and can never fully possess. events.title: Mozart’s “Jupiter”
events.description: Utah Symphony Music Director Designate Markus Poschner leads an all-Mozart program starring Concertmaster Madeline Adkins in Mozart’s second violin concerto. Written when he was just 18, the concerto showcases effortless virtuosity with the elegance and clarity that would continue to define his voice throughout his career. events.title: Mozart, Prokofiev, & More
events.description: Mozart's gripping Overture to Don Giovanni opens the concert with ominous chords and cleverly placed silence, building tension before racing forward with exhilarating momentum. Prokofiev&\#03's sparkling Sinfonietta follows, bursting with playful rhythms colored by the composer&\#03's youthful wit and charm. The program concludes with Louise Farrenc's Symphony No. 3, an expressive work admired in her lifetime by musicians including Schumann and Berlioz for its elegant structure and dramatic range, moving between brooding drama and romantic warmth.

You can mix and match which races you would like to sign up for or get all three at an amazing price! Plus, you can 10% per person by signing up 4 or more people for the series. events.title: Michael Kessler: All Resin
events.description: Michael Kessler has spent decades exploring the boundary between gesture and geometry, and his resin works bring that inquiry into sharper focus than ever. Each painting is built from up to fifty micro-thin translucent layers, fused and accumulated until something luminous emerges. Biomorphic forms weave through geometric structures, nature's logic made visible through an almost meditative process of application and restraint. Kessler likens his gestural freedom to a kind of painterly tai chi, controlled, but never rigid. In his all-resin works, that layered depth takes on new dimension. ...

events.title: Beethoven’s Symphony No. 7
events.description: Promising a night of lyrical beauty and orchestral drama, conductor Louis Lohraseb shapes a program that follows an arc of adoration to heartbreak in the first half. The season finale at St. Mary’s Catholic Church opens with Wagner’s tender Siegfried Idyll, a deeply personal expression of love written as a birthday gift for his wife. Then the spotlight turns to Erica Petrocelli in two dramatic works that showcase the brilliance and expressive power of the soprano voice: Mozart’s virtuosic “Come scoglio” from Così fan tutte, a declaration of unwavering fidelity, and Mendelsohn's “Infelice,” ...

events.description: Two young musicians from Nashville, TN. Max Pachter and Evan deNicola of the "Eastern Dreamers" bring 70s Folk and Americana to life with their unique guitar-based approach to their performances and songwriting. Max's raw and smoky voice paired with Evan's pure and heady tenor create a contrasting, but soothing dynamic when harmonizing together. Paying homage in their sound to the likes of "Simon & Garfunkel" and "Crosby, Stills, and Nash", their authentic, heartfelt storytelling along with their clear musical abilities create a deeply awe-inspiring and intimate experience for their listeners.

Every November, Live PC Give PC brings our community together through LivePCGivePC.org for 24 hours of extreme generosity. Children break open their piggy banks. Young people dip into their savings. Businesses kick in matching grants. With everyone in the community giving whatever they can, we’ve raised millions of dollars for local nonprofits. It’s how we’re working together to preserve the place we love—so it will continue to thrive for generations to come.

This workshop reflects Mountain Mediation’s commitment to peaceful, cost‑conscious conflict resolution. events.description: Shepherd of the Mountains Lutheran Church will be hosting a blood drive with the American Red Cross on Friday December 4th, from 1:00 PM – 6:00 PM at Shepherd of the Mountains Lutheran Church located at 4051 N Highway 224, just north of Canyons Resort Drive. **Appointments are required**. If you are eligible to donate, please make an appointment at **www.redcrossblood.org** or call 1-800-733-2767 to set up an appointment.

events.title: secrets below alta: wild west archaeology in a mining boom town
events.description: secrets below alta: wild west archaeology in a mining boom town Thursday, January 14th | Our Lady of the Snows | 6PM | Free | Sign-up Required If you love Old West ghost-town history, you won't want to miss this! For the first time ever, the mining boomtown of Alta has been partially excavated, and what emerged from the ground is incredible. Thousands of artifacts were uncovered, including a fully intact bottle of 150-year-old alcohol, a leather hat, bullets, melted shot glasses, animal bones, and so much more. Discover what these objects reveal about the rowdy and genteel past of Little Co...
events.location: Our Lady of the Snows Center, Alta, UT
